title: Everything you need to know about your Back Market wallet
bodyHtml:
Overview of your wallet
 

Your wallet serves to equip you with all the details you need to grow your business. When you know the dates and total amounts of your payouts, you can plan better and smarter.

 Navigate to your wallet by going to the "Invoices" tab in your Back Office

Here you’ll find 3 useful features:

  • Your wallet balance
  • Your upcoming payouts
  • Your deferred payout




Your wallet balance is the amount you currently have. If you sell in markets with different currencies, you’ll see them listed here.

Your upcoming payouts section details the dates and amounts of your upcoming payouts.

And your deferred payout is the amount of money Back Market holds to cover any issues that might arise during your devices’ warranty periods. (Don’t worry, it’s not much.)
 

The details about your wallet balance


Depending on which countries you sell in, your wallet might show different currencies. It displays a certain percentage of your total sales. (The remaining amount is held as a deferred payout. Keep reading for more details.)


 

The details about your upcoming payouts


As you know, payouts are issued 3 times a month — on the 6th, 16th, and 26th.

On the 16th of the month, you’re paid what you sold between the 26th of the last month and 6th of the current month, minus what has been refunded between the 6th and the 16th of the current month.

On the 26th of the month, you're paid what you sold between the 6th and the 16th of the current month, minus what has been refunded between the 16th and the 26th of the current month.

For more details on your payouts or invoices, check out this article

In the upcoming payouts section, you’ll see your 2 future payouts. Again, if you sell in markets with different currencies, you’ll see them here too.



You’ll be able to track your selling periods and know exactly how much you’ve sold in different currencies. Please note that this section is updated everyday, so the numbers will fluctuate depending on your activity.
 

The details about your deferred payout


Here you’ll see the amount held back by Back Market. This amount serves to cover any issues that might happen during your items’ warranty periods.



If you want to know more about why we hold your deferred payout and how this number is calculated, check out this article
